question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network-adjacent position perform a DNS hijack against the default endpoint resolved by `URI.create(sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint())` when `sourceProperties.getUri()` is null, redirecting S3 stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server that serves tampered transaction record files, causing the mirror node to ingest falsified transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AnonymousCredentialsProvider` path (when `isAnonymousCredentials()` returns true) to write tampered transaction stream files to a publicly writable S3 bucket, since anonymous access implies no write-side authentication check is enforced by the client configuration, causing the importer to download and process attacker-supplied transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate pinning or hostname verification in the `NettyNioAsyncHttpClient` built inside `s3Client()` to perform a man-in-the-middle attack against the S3 endpoint, injecting tampered transaction record stream files in transit, causing the mirror node to store and serve falsified transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the `projectId` value (e.g., via environment variable injection or misconfigured external config) inject arbitrary characters into the raw query string via `appendRawQueryParameter(\

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `forcePathStyle(true)` combined with a manipulated `bucketName` containing path-separator characters (e.g., `legit-bucket/../../attacker-bucket`) to cause the S3AsyncClient to resolve object keys against an attacker-controlled bucket path, resulting in tampered transaction files being downloaded and ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject a `LOCAL` source type entry into `commonDownloaderProperties.getSources()` cause `LocalStreamFileProvider` to be instantiated and read transaction stream files from an attacker-controlled local filesystem path, bypassing S3 integrity checks and allowing tampered transaction data to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri())` call with a URI pointing to an HTTP (non-TLS) endpoint to perform a plaintext interception attack, replacing legitimate transaction stream files with tampered ones in transit, since the `NettyNioAsyncHttpClient` does not enforce HTTPS-only connections?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DefaultCredentialsProvider` fallback path (when neither anonymous nor static credentials are configured) to leverage a misconfigured EC2 instance metadata service (IMDS) or environment variable that the attacker has poisoned, obtaining credentials that allow writing tampered transaction files to the source bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `connectionMaxIdleTime(Duration.ofSeconds(5))` setting to force frequent TCP connection recycling, enabling a timing-based connection hijack where a new connection is established to an attacker-controlled endpoint between idle timeout and reconnect, delivering tampered transaction stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the `sources` list in `CommonDownloaderProperties` add a duplicate or shadow source entry pointing to an attacker-controlled S3-compatible endpoint, causing `streamFileProviders()` to return a provider that fetches tampered transaction files alongside legitimate ones, polluting the consensus verification pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response integrity verification in the `S3AsyncClient` built by `s3Client()` — specifically the absence of any checksum or signature validation on downloaded objects — to serve bit-flipped or structurally modified transaction record files from a compromised CDN or cache layer, causing the importer to process tampered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the anonymous `ExecutionInterceptor` created inside `overrideConfiguration()` — which modifies every outgoing HTTP request by appending `userProject` — to cause request smuggling if `projectId` contains URL-encoded characters like `%26` or `%3D`, altering the effective S3 request target and fetching tampered transaction files from an unintended location?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Region.of(sourceProperties.getRegion())` call with an attacker-influenced region string to cause the S3 client to resolve a region-specific endpoint that the attacker controls (e.g., a fake AWS region endpoint via DNS), redirecting transaction stream file downloads to a tampered source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user who can submit crafted configuration (e.g., via an exposed actuator endpoint or environment variable) set `pathPrefix` to a value that traverses into an attacker-controlled bucket prefix, causing `S3StreamFileProvider` to list and download tampered transaction files from that prefix instead of the legitimate node stream path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StaticCredentialsProvider.create(AwsBasicCredentials.create(credentials.getAccessKey(), credentials.getSecretKey()))` path by observing that static credentials are logged or exposed (since `log.info` is called before credential creation), then using those credentials to overwrite legitimate transaction stream files in the S3 bucket with tampered versions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `maxConcurrency(sourceProperties.getMaxConcurrency())` value of 1000 to flood the S3 endpoint with concurrent requests, causing legitimate stream file downloads to time out while an attacker-controlled mirror endpoint (set via DNS poisoning) responds faster, resulting in tampered transaction files being preferentially ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `connectionTimeout(sourceProperties.getConnectionTimeout())` of 5 seconds by making the legitimate S3 endpoint artificially slow (via a volumetric attack), causing the client to fail over to a secondary source that the attacker has pre-positioned with tampered transaction stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `streamFileProviders()` iterates over all configured sources without any integrity cross-check between them, such that if one source returns tampered transaction files and another returns legitimate ones, the downstream consensus ratio check uses both, allowing a sufficiently large attacker-controlled source set to tip the ratio and accept tampered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of S3 object versioning enforcement in the `GetObjectRequest` built by the downstream `S3StreamFileProvider` (using the client from `s3Client()`) to perform a version-rollback attack, replacing current transaction stream files with older versions that omit or alter specific transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a newline character (`\\n` or `\\r\\n`) into the `projectId` value used in `appendRawQueryParameter(\

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `forcePathStyle(true)` configuration to perform a bucket name injection attack where a crafted bucket name containing `/` characters causes the path-style URL to resolve to a different bucket or key prefix, fetching tampered transaction stream files from an attacker-controlled S3 path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AnonymousCredentialsProvider` path to enumerate all transaction stream files in a publicly accessible bucket (since no authentication is required), identify gaps or patterns in the stream, and then upload tampered files to fill those gaps if the bucket also allows anonymous writes, causing the importer to ingest falsified transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `GCP` source type path in `streamFileProviders()` — which forces `isStaticCredentials()` to return true regardless of whether credentials are set — to cause the client to use empty or null GCP credentials, resulting in unauthenticated access to a GCS bucket where the attacker has pre-uploaded tampered transaction stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of SSRF protection in `endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri())` — where the URI is accepted without validation of scheme, host, or port — to redirect S3 requests to an internal metadata service (e.g., `http://169.254.169.254`) or attacker-controlled internal host, exfiltrating credentials that are then used to tamper with transaction stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `NettyNioAsyncHttpClient` with `maxConcurrency(1000)` to perform a connection pool exhaustion attack by opening 1000 slow connections to the S3 endpoint, preventing legitimate transaction stream file downloads and creating a window during which a fallback or retry mechanism fetches from an attacker-controlled source?
